column4: to log a new networking connection after a conversation
column5: I do not forget the person or the conversation summary of our discussion
column6:
Given I have completed a networking conversation
When I enter the contact’s name and name required; notes optional
Then the connection is saved and visible in my contact list

Given a connection is saved
When I return to the app later
Then I can see the contact details and notes

The system stores the date the connection was created

column4: to set an intended follow-up date for a connection
column5: I can turn good intentions into a concrete commitment
column6:
Given a saved connection
When I choose a follow-up timeframe
Then the commitment is associated with that contact

Given I have set a timeframe
When I view the contact
Then I can clearly see the scheduled follow-up date

The follow-up date can be updated if needed

column4: to receive an in-app and/or notification reminder before my follow-up is due
column5: I complete follow-ups within my intended timeframe
column6:
Given a follow-up is scheduled
X time before the scheduled follow-up date
Then I receive a notification

Given I receive a reminder
When I open the app
Then it opens the contact detail page for that connection

Reminders are tied only to scheduled follow-ups

column4: to mark a follow-up as complete
column5: I can track that I followed through on my commitment
column6:
Given a scheduled follow-up
When I complete outreach
Then I can mark it as complete

Given it is marked complete
Then it is removed from the upcoming/overdue follow-up list

Completion auto-records completion date/time

column4: to see a list of upcoming follow-ups
column5: I know what relationships need attention soon
column6:
Given I open the app
When I view my dashboard
Then I see upcoming follow-ups sorted by soonest follow-up date first

Overdue follow-ups are clearly indicated

Completed follow-ups are excluded from the default view

column4: to see when a follow-up is overdue
column5: I can take action before relationships fade
column6:
Given a follow-up when the scheduled follow-up date/time is earlier than the current date/time
When I open the app
Then the item is labeled overdue

Overdue follow-ups appear in an 'Overdue' section/list

column4: to store conversation notes and next steps
column5: I can reference context during future outreach
column6:
Given a saved connection
When I add notes
Then the notes are stored with that contact

Notes can be edited later

Notes are visible on the contact detail page and when opening the reminder for that contact during future follow-ups

column5: I feel confident I am maintaining important relationships
column6:
Given I have logged connections
When I view my progress summary
Then I can see the (number of follow-ups completed on or before the scheduled follow-up date) / (number of follow-ups whose scheduled due date falls within the period)

The calculation reflects only completed vs. scheduled follow-ups

The metric updates when follow-ups are marked complete

column4: to schedule follow-ups more than 30 days in the future
column5: I can maintain long-term professional relationships
column6:
Given I am setting a follow-up
When I select a date more than 30 days away
Then the system accepts and stores the date

Then the follow-up appears immediately in the follow-up list, even if it's 30+ days away

Reminders follow the same reminder rule as Story 3 and keeps testing consistent

column4: to reschedule a follow-up if circumstances change
column5: I can maintain realistic commitments without losing the relationship
column6:
Given a scheduled follow-up
When I change the date
Then the new date replaces the old date on the connection page

The updated date triggers reminders appropriately

If a follow-up is overdue and I reschedule it to a future date, it is no longer labeled overdue

The change is reflected immediately in my dashboard

After rescheduling, 'on-time' is evaluated against the original due date / most recent due date

column4: to manage multiple networking connections at once
column5: I do not lose track during recruiting seasons
column6:
Given I log multiple contacts
When I view my connection list
Then all connections with a pending follow-up are displayed

Each connection maintains its own follow-up schedule

Marking one follow-up complete doesn't change the status or dates of other connections

column4: to see evidence that I am consistently following through
column5: I see evidence that I am maintaining relationships
column6:
Given I complete follow-ups on time
When I review my progress
Then I can clearly see counts/trends of completed follow-ups and on-time completion rate

Progress reflects adherence to my original timeframe

The summary reinforces completed commitments

column4: to see a list of completed follow-ups
column5: I can reflect on maintained relationships
column6:
Given completed follow-ups exist
When I access my history from a progress summary
Then I see a list of completed follow-ups

Each item includes the completion date and what identifies the item. Each item shows connection name and completion date

Completed follow-ups appear in a Completed section/list and don't appear in the Pending/Upcoming/Overdue list

column4: to return to long-term follow-ups when due date is reached or X amount of days prior to due date
column5: I maintain engagement over extended timelines
column6:
Given a follow-up is scheduled more than 30 days ahead of the scheduled due date
When the reminder occurs 5 days before
Then I am prompted to re-engage with the given connection

The follow-up remains visible in the upcoming list and contact list until completed

Engagement is recorded when I act on it

column4: to remove a connection that is no longer relevant
column5: my list stays focused and manageable
column6:
Given a saved connection
When I choose to delete it
Then it is removed from my follow-up list

Deleted connections no longer appear in upcoming follow-ups and are automatically removed

Other connections remain unaffected
